metadata.title:
v. v. P. TRYING


To BUTT IN

pages.raw_text:
Just what the intention of the
Washington Water Power Co. was
in filing with the board of works
yesterday a protest against the al
legged damming of the river, is a
mystery to city officials, but is
taken to be a legal warning In case
the river should later on be
damned to such an extent that it
would hinder the power of the falls.
The communication states that the
river has been damned with rocks
and sacks OF sand, and the com
pany protests on the ground that
backwater will damage property.


The fact Is that 500 yards of
rock has been removed from the
river. Sandbags have been sunk
there, though, but will be removed
In few days, as by that time the
piers and abuttments will be ready
for the concrete. A significant
fact is that the Traction Co., which
will be required to pay one half OF
the expense with the city. is salts
fed with the progress of the work.
and Chief Engineer Zimmerman, of
the company, is constantly on the
job, watching for any mistake in
construction. The w. w. p. does
not Mse the bridge.


The w. w. p. Co. claims owner
ship Of the river and general SUI
pervision of its banks. The com
pany was anxious that no publicity
be given its latest assertion of
proprietorship.
